description: A layered ecological planting designed to create the sense of moving through a small landscape rather than viewing a traditional garden border. A gentle stepping-stone path passes through the bed, allowing moments of immersion within the planting, while the surrounding vegetation builds structure, softness, and seasonal change. Taller shrubs and small trees form a protective woodland backdrop along the far edge, while grasses and flowering perennials create a flowing meadow-like matrix through the middle. Lower groundcovers soften the path edge and create a continuous living ground layer. The intention is a small ecological landscape -- soft, natural, and immersive -- where structure, habitat, and seasonal beauty work together.

habitat.planting_method: Layered ecological planting -- stepping-stone path design

activity_log.title: Ecological Cottage Garden -- Design
activity_log.notes: A layered ecological planting designed to create the sense of moving through a small landscape. A stepping-stone path passes through the bed allowing moments of immersion. Taller shrubs and small trees form a woodland backdrop -- Elaeocarpus, Hakea, Leptospermum and Olearia establish the upper layer. Through the middle, grasses including Themeda, Poa and Dichelachne create a flowing matrix, while flowering perennials emerge through to provide cottage seasonal colour. Closer to the path, Myoporum, Dichondra, Pratia and Scaevola soften the ground plane. The bed responds to varying light conditions -- shade-tolerant shrubs behind the path, sun-loving grasses and wildflowers in the open areas.

notes: Design proposal stage. Baseline score will be recorded post-installation. Full garden stocktake of all beds planned at a later date -- this record covers the primary ecological cottage garden bed only (16x8m).
